axiom: Stream what the user needs to see — final tokens for chat, intermediate updates for agents, custom events for progress — over the right transport, and decide up front whether a client disconnect cancels or detaches the run.

audiences.name: agent-inspector
audiences.wants: intermediate step updates (node entered, tool called, partial state)

audiences.name: progress
audiences.wants: in-tool/in-node progress invisible to the framework

operations.trigger: User-facing chat; want typing effect on the final answer.
operations.action: LangGraph graph.astream(inp, stream_mode='messages') -> yield each (token, metadata).content, filtered to the final node. Raw: OpenAI stream=True iterate delta.content; Anthropic with client.messages.stream(...) as s: for t in s.text_stream.
operations.output: Character-by-character final answer; no tool JSON leaks.
operations.limitations: Without metadata filtering, multi-agent graphs leak sub-agent tokens into the bubble (see op-8).

operations.trigger: Dev/inspector UI; show which node/tool with inputs.
operations.action: LangGraph stream_mode='updates' -> each chunk is {node_name: state_diff}; render as step log. LCEL astream_events(version='v2'), switch on event['event'] (on_tool_start/on_tool_end/on_chain_*).
operations.output: Live step trace without the full-state weight of 'values'.
operations.limitations: Raw OpenAI/Anthropic have no first-class step stream; reconstruct from tool-call deltas / content-block events.

operations.trigger: A tool/node does long internal work (embed 200 docs, paginate API) the framework cannot see.
operations.action: LangGraph: inside node, w = get_stream_writer(); w({'progress': i/n}); consume on stream_mode='custom'. LCEL: dispatch a custom event/callback surfaced by astream_events.
operations.output: Real progress signal instead of a frozen spinner.
operations.limitations: OpenAI/Anthropic have no in-call progress channel; must emit out-of-band.

operations.trigger: One surface needs tokens AND steps AND progress on one connection.
operations.action: stream_mode=['messages','updates','custom']; map each (mode, chunk) tuple to a distinct SSE event: name; client addEventListener per name.
operations.output: Single connection, cleanly demuxed; no extra round-trips.
operations.limitations: Client must implement the demux; untagged interleaving is unparseable.

operations.action: Receive-only -> SSE (free reconnect + Last-Event-ID). Client must push mid-stream (cancel/steer/collab) -> WebSocket. Both ends yours / non-HTTP -> async generator.
operations.output: Right transport; no hand-rolled reconnect for the common case.
operations.limitations: WebSocket adds reconnect logic SSE gives for free; don't reach for it by default.

operations.trigger: Stream may outlive the client's interest.
operations.action: Hook the request cancellation token. CANCEL: close the async generator -> upstream LLM/agent call aborts; release resources. DETACH: keep running under a checkpointer, log events with monotonic IDs so reconnect replays from Last-Event-ID.
operations.output: No zombie runs burning tokens; or a resumable run, by design.
operations.limitations: Detach requires a persistence layer (defer to [[langgraph-sop]] Step 6).

operations.trigger: Long quiet gaps cause proxy drops, or tokens arrive in clumps not smoothly.
operations.action: Emit ': ping\n\n' comments every ~15s; set X-Accel-Buffering: no / disable proxy buffering; flush after each event.
operations.output: Connection survives idle periods; tokens render smoothly.
operations.limitations: Heartbeat interval must beat the proxy idle timeout.

operations.trigger: A multi-agent graph streams every LLM's tokens; the chat bubble fills with sub-agent noise.
operations.action: On messages mode, inspect metadata (langgraph_node, tags) and forward only tokens whose node is the user-facing responder; route the rest to updates (dev view) or drop.
operations.output: Clean final answer; sub-agent reasoning stays in the inspector.
operations.limitations: Requires knowing the responder node name / tag convention.

dilemma_cases.name: Stream tokens vs stream steps for an agent that thinks then answers
dilemma_cases.tension: messages-only -> 40s frozen spinner then text dump; updates-only -> step list but final answer arrives all at once losing the typing feel.
dilemma_cases.resolution: stream_mode=['updates','messages']: updates drive a live step list during tool work; messages tokens stream into the bubble for the final node (op-4 demux + op-8 filter). Add custom progress for slow individual tools (op-3).
dilemma_cases.rule: The answer to 'tokens or steps' is almost always 'both, tagged, on one wire' — the question is which is primary when.

dilemma_cases.name: Client disconnects mid-stream — cancel run or finish?
dilemma_cases.tension: 90s agent with an irreversible side effect (books a flight); user closes tab at second 30. Hard-cancel risks a half-booking; finishing burns tokens for a possibly-gone client.
dilemma_cases.resolution: Decision = cancel-vs-detach by reversibility + cost. Side-effecting: detach, let the durable step finish under a checkpointer, persist event log with monotonic IDs, replay on reconnect via Last-Event-ID. Read-only chat: cancel immediately to stop burning tokens.
dilemma_cases.rule: Disconnect policy is a function of side-effect reversibility and per-step cost — decide it per surface, before shipping; never let it default to whatever the framework does on socket close.

anti_patterns.name: Stream everything
anti_patterns.symptom: Forwarding raw debug/values firehose to a chat UI floods it with full-state snapshots and sub-agent tokens.
anti_patterns.fix: Project to the audience (Step 2); values is heavy by design.

anti_patterns.name: Stream a sub-second call
anti_patterns.symptom: SSE/WS framing + reconnect + partial-parse bugs for zero perceived-latency gain.
anti_patterns.fix: Return the whole payload; only stream when a human waits on a >1-2s run.

anti_patterns.name: No disconnect handling
anti_patterns.symptom: Zombie runs burn tokens after the client is gone, or side effects half-complete.
anti_patterns.fix: Decide cancel vs detach in Step 5 (op-6).

anti_patterns.name: Untagged interleaved streams
anti_patterns.symptom: Tokens and tool names on one unnamed wire are unparseable client-side.
anti_patterns.fix: Tag with SSE event: / the (mode, chunk) tuple (op-4).

anti_patterns.name: Sub-agent token leak
anti_patterns.symptom: Final chat bubble fills with internal reasoning from non-responder LLMs.

anti_patterns.name: No heartbeat
anti_patterns.symptom: Long quiet gaps behind a proxy silently kill the connection; user sees a hang.
anti_patterns.fix: Ping every ~15s with a comment line (op-7).

anti_patterns.name: Proxy buffering left on
anti_patterns.symptom: Tokens arrive batched, destroying the streaming feel.
anti_patterns.fix: Disable buffering explicitly (X-Accel-Buffering: no), flush per event (op-7).

anti_patterns.name: WebSocket by default
anti_patterns.symptom: Hand-rolling reconnect for a receive-only surface.
anti_patterns.fix: Use SSE; reserve WS for true bidirectional needs (op-5).

boundaries: Output must be validated/transformed as a whole before the user sees any of it (structured JSON, safety pass) -> stream into a parser, never raw to the user.
Consumer is a machine wanting one atomic JSON object -> return the whole response; partial JSON tokens are a parsing hazard.
No human waiting (offline batch) -> streaming adds cost for no audience.

cross_framework.langgraph: Cleanest separable projections (messages/updates/custom) composable on one wire; best when you already have a graph. See [[langgraph-sop]] OP-8.
cross_framework.langchain_lcel: astream_events = richest typed event taxonomy; one stream, branch on event. Best for fine-grained lifecycle routing without a full graph.
cross_framework.openai: Single token/delta stream; steps reconstructed from tool_calls deltas; supports cancellation.
cross_framework.anthropic: client.messages.stream + text_stream; tool use via content-block events; steps reconstructed.
cross_framework.transport_note: SSE / WebSocket / async generator is orthogonal to the SDK; pick SDK for what to stream, transport for how the client consumes.
